Miner Retirement Identity Guard

RCA and release-candidate mitigation for the 2026-05-24 Athena count mismatch: the pool had five active miners while dashboard/audit displayed four.

Failure

Retired IP fallback hid an active Stratum client at 192.168.1.102.

Root Cause

The filter treated an IP address as persistent ASIC identity. IP addresses and workers are ephemeral observations; only MAC address is a permanent miner identity.

Mitigation

Only a MAC match can retire an ASIC. IP and worker matches are kept as observations and cannot suppress an active miner by themselves.

Evidence

SignalObservation
Pool metricspool_active_connections=5, pool_job_health_authorized_miners=5, and pool_job_health_ready_miners=5.
Runtime filterminer-retirements.json still contained Athena with old MAC 28:e2:97:4c:e4:0a and IP 192.168.1.102.
Current active observationsAthena was submitting from 192.168.1.102 with current neighbor MAC 2a:71:c7:f5:1f:1e and current pool worker 0x05518E03e148C56e426ff9e1CBdB962B4FC5250A.
Immediate fixThe stale Athena retirement row was removed from live ops/runtime/miner-retirements.json; audit returned to five tracked, connected, and ok miners.

Release-Candidate Fix

FileChange
ops/pool_ops.pyAdded structured retired-miner identity decisions. A same MAC retires the miner. IP and worker observations never retire, suppress, reintroduce, rename, or mark a miner as a new device by themselves.
ops/tests/test_miner_retirement_identity.pyRegression tests cover active different MAC at an observed retired IP, authoritative same-MAC retirement, IP-without-MAC observation behavior, and worker-without-MAC observation behavior.
scripts/validate-release-build.shRelease validation keeps the release-build contract focused on the bootstrap and runtime payload path; MAC-only identity coverage remains in the source tests.
/home/jeremy/blockdag-source/SOURCE_ACCESS_IMPROVEMENT_BACKLOG.mdBacklog item STACK-P1-MINER-RETIREMENT-IP-CONFLICT-20260524 records RCA, owner, acceptance criteria, and evidence.

Acceptance Criteria

CaseExpected Behavior
Same MAC as a retired rowRemain retired and hidden from local miner accounting.
Same IP without MAC evidenceKeep the miner visible, count it, and emit a warning because IP is only an observation.
Same worker without MAC evidenceKeep the miner visible. Worker labels are accounting/routing observations, not ASIC identity.
Different MAC at an observed retired IPKeep the miner visible, count it, and emit a warning so the operator can reconcile retirement metadata.

Related Count Guard

CaseExpected Behavior
Tracked unmanaged miners with status=okContribute to miner_health.ok_count.
Managed-only health countExpose separately as managed_ok_count so the public ok_count label matches visible miner row status.
